feat: isinstance() builtin support with mp_obj_is_type() emission (#42)

* feat: isinstance() builtin support with mp_obj_is_type() emission

Add compile-time isinstance() that emits efficient mp_obj_is_type() C
calls for runtime type dispatch. Supports class hierarchies, traits,
@dataclass variants, and negated checks. Enables MVU-style sealed trait
message dispatch pattern (isinstance + manual type narrowing).

- IsInstanceIR node in IR pipeline
- _build_isinstance() in IR builder with validation
- _emit_isinstance() in function emitter -> mp_obj_is_type()
- IR visualizer support for isinstance nodes
- isinstance_demo.py example (shapes + MVU messages)
- 15 unit tests, 3 C runtime integration tests
- Device test suite for ESP32-C6 verification
- Mock runtime updates (mp_obj_is_type, mp_obj_new_bool, arg parsing)

* feat: automatic type narrowing after isinstance() checks

After `if isinstance(x, Dog):`, the variable `x` is automatically
treated as Dog-typed inside the if-body, enabling direct struct field
access without manual `d: Dog = x` annotations. Supports negated
isinstance (narrows in else branch), elif chains, and method context.

- _extract_isinstance_narrowing() helper detects isinstance in if-test AST
- _build_if/_build_method_if save/restore _class_typed_params for scoped narrowing
- isinstance_demo.py updated to use auto-narrowing (no manual annotations)
- 10 unit tests, 3 C runtime integration tests for auto-narrowing
- Device test comments updated for auto-narrowing

* docs: update feature scope and roadmap for isinstance, auto-narrowing, IntEnum, and special methods

* Update CHANGELOG.md

commit.message:
feat: add Windows dual-boot support

UEFI: chain-loads bootmgfw.efi via LoadImage + StartImage
- Scans boot volume first, then all SFS volumes
- Sets DeviceHandle so Windows can find its BCD store
- StartImage hands off directly; ExitBootServices is not
  called by SakuruBoot (Windows does it itself)
- BitLocker, Fast Startup, and BCD are fully transparent
- Custom kernel path supported (optional, auto-detected by default)

BIOS: VBR chain-load from active NTFS partition
- Reads MBR partition table, finds active NTFS/FAT32 partition
- Copies 512-byte VBR to 0x7C00 and jumps to it with DL=drive
- Falls back to first NTFS partition if none are flagged active

Config:
- New OS_TYPE_WINDOWS enum value in common/config.h
- type = windows in sakuru.cfg
- config_guess_type() detects bootmgfw.efi / bootmgr.efi
- sakuru.cfg.example updated with Windows entries

Docs:
- New docs/pages/docs/windows.md: full install guide including
  partitioning, restoring SakuruBoot after Windows install,
  efibootmgr registration, BitLocker/Fast Startup notes,
  troubleshooting table
- docs sidebar updated with Windows dual-boot link
- Landing page feature grid updated (7 cards now)
- README features list updated

commit.message:
CFL-076: cap NamedColor2 nDeviceCoords to 16 — CWE-400 timeout fix

CIccTagNamedColor2::Read() accepted unbounded nDeviceCoords (20M+ from
crafted profile), causing O(nCoords) iteration in Describe() via snprintf
loop. ICC spec limits color spaces to 16 channels max (NChannelData).

Root cause: nCoords read from profile without range validation. Existing
allocation cap (CFL-004, 128MB) passed because nSize=1 × large entry.

Fix: reject nCoords > 16 in Read() before allocation.
Result: >30s → 0ms.

PoC: timeout-0bec9575 (323 bytes, ncl2 tag with nCoords=20734320)
Upstream iccDumpProfile handles PoC fine (treats ncn1 as unknown tag).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(fsi): add --multiemit- to prevent cross-submission TypeLoadException

FSI's default --multiemit+ creates separate dynamic assemblies per
EvalInteraction call. MonoMod's JIT hook (loaded by Expecto via Harmony)
throws TypeLoadException when types from one assembly are referenced in
another. This made records, DUs, and with-expressions fail across REPL
submissions.

Fix: add --multiemit- to FSI args in both code paths (solutionToFsiArgs
and DirectoryConfig eval). All submissions now share one dynamic assembly,
allowing types to be referenced across submissions.

Trade-off: types cannot be REDEFINED in subsequent submissions (duplicate
type name error), but for a REPL tool with finite session lifetimes and
hard-reset capability, this is the right trade-off.

13 TDD tests: 3 unit (args correctness) + 5 integration (record,
with-expression, DU, anon record, function) + 5 property (FsCheck:
N-field records, N-case DUs, int bindings, N-field anon records,
with-expression preservation).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Security hardening Phase 1 — critical auth & session fixes (#27, #28, #29, #33, #38) (#51)

* fix: security hardening — session ID entropy, XFF spoofing, rate limiter cap, timing attack, session resume auth (#27, #28, #29, #33, #38)

Phase 1 security fixes with TDD:

- #33: Replace mt19937 PRNG with direct std::random_device reads (128-bit
  OS CSPRNG entropy). Extract to server/session_id.hpp.
- #28: Extract IP extraction to server/ip_extraction.hpp with trust_proxy
  flag. Only reads X-Forwarded-For when trust_proxy=true, takes rightmost
  IP. Default: socket address only.
- #29: Add max_tracked_ips cap and evict_to_cap() to AuthRateLimiter.
  Evicts expired entries first, then lowest-failure entries. Add size()
  accessor.
- #27: Add constant-time string comparison (server/constant_time.hpp) for
  API key validation. Prevents timing side-channel attacks.
- #38: Validate session exists in SessionManager before allowing resume.
  Reject resume requests for non-existent sessions.

23 new tests (232 → 255), all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR #51 review feedback — 18 comments resolved

Review fixes:
- constant_time: XOR length mismatch into result instead of early return
  (prevents key length leak via timing). Add volatile rationale comment,
  <cstddef> include, use std::size_t.
- session_id: Use 4 rd() calls extracting 4 bytes each (4× more efficient).
  Replace ostringstream with zero-allocation hex table.
- ip_extraction: Add trusted_hops parameter for multi-proxy deployments.
  Trim HTAB per RFC 7239 OWS. Document single-proxy assumption.
- auth_rate_limiter: Compute return value BEFORE eviction to avoid dangling
  reference UB. Switch eviction to oldest window_start (LRU-like) to prevent
  bypass via distributed low-failure IPs. Fix comment/code mismatch.
- websocket_server: Fix inverted session resume check — reject if session ID
  already exists (conflict), not if it doesn't. Wire trust_proxy,
  trusted_proxy_hops, max_tracked_ips through config.hpp and main.cpp with
  WSS_TRUST_PROXY, WSS_TRUSTED_PROXY_HOPS, WSS_MAX_TRACKED_IPS env vars.
- test_session_id: Add missing <cctype> include.
- test_ip_extraction: Add multi-proxy and HTAB whitespace tests.
- test_rate_limiter_capacity: Adapt to oldest-eviction strategy.

258 tests passing (232 baseline + 26 new).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
